questions.question: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), health is defined as:
options: A state of complete physical, mental and social well-being
Being able to perform daily tasks
questions.explanation: Health does not simply mean ‘absence of disease’ or ‘physical fitness’. It is defined as a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is a bacterial disease in humans?
questions.explanation: Salmonella typhi is a pathogenic bacterium which causes typhoid fever in human beings.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The Widal test is used for the susceptibility/confirmation of which disease?
questions.explanation: Typhoid fever could be confirmed by Widal test.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which bacteria are responsible for causing pneumonia in humans?
questions.explanation: Bacteria like Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae are responsible for the disease pneumonia in humans.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The common cold is caused by which group of viruses?
questions.explanation: Rhinoviruses represent one such group of viruses which cause one of the most infectious human ailments – the common cold.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The infectious stage of Plasmodium that enters the human body through a mosquito bite is:
questions.explanation: Plasmodium enters the human body as sporozoites (infectious form) through the bite of infected female Anopheles mosquito.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Malignant malaria is caused by which species of Plasmodium?
questions.explanation: Malignant malaria caused by Plasmodium falciparum is the most serious one and can even be fatal.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The rupture of RBCs in malaria releases a toxic substance responsible for chill and high fever called:
questions.explanation: The parasites multiply within the liver cells and then attack the red blood cells resulting in their rupture. The rupture of RBCs is associated with release of a toxic substance, haemozoin.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Amoebiasis (amoebic dysentery) is caused by which protozoan parasite?
questions.explanation: Entamoeba histolytica is a protozoan parasite in the large intestine of human which causes amoebiasis.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Ascariasis is caused by an intestinal parasite known as:
questions.explanation: Ascaris, the common roundworm and Wuchereria, the filarial worm, are some of the helminths which are known to be pathogenic to man. Ascaris, an intestinal parasite causes ascariasis.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which disease is characterized by the chronic inflammation of lymphatic vessels, usually of the lower limbs?
questions.explanation: Wuchereria (W. bancrofti and W. malayi), the filarial worms cause a slowly developing chronic inflammation of the organs in which they live for many years, usually the lymphatic vessels of the lower limbs.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Ringworm is caused by which of the following genera of fungi?
questions.explanation: Many fungi belonging to the genera Microsporum, Trichophyton and Epidermophyton are responsible for ringworms.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Innate immunity is a ________ type of defense.
questions.explanation: Innate immunity is non-specific type of defence, that is present at the time of birth.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Interferons are a type of ________ barrier in innate immunity.
questions.explanation: Virus-infected cells secrete proteins called interferons which protect non-infected cells from further viral infection. These are cytokine barriers.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Acquired immunity is characterized by:
questions.explanation: Acquired immunity... is pathogen specific. It is characterised by memory.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Humoral immune response is mediated by antibodies produced by:
questions.explanation: The B-lymphocytes produce an army of proteins in response to pathogens into our blood to fight with them. These proteins are called antibodies. This is called humoral immune response.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Cell-mediated immunity (CMI) is responsible for graft rejection and is mediated by:
questions.explanation: T-lymphocytes mediate CMI. Cell-mediated immune response is responsible for the graft rejection.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of passive immunity?
options: Colostrum (IgA) received by an infant from mother
questions.explanation: The yellowish fluid colostrum secreted by mother during the initial days of lactation has abundant antibodies (IgA) to protect the infant. This is an example of passive immunity.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Allergies involve the exaggerated response of the immune system to certain antigens, mediated by which antibody?
questions.explanation: The antibodies produced to these (allergens) are of IgE type.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Rheumatoid arthritis is an example of:
questions.explanation: Sometimes, due to genetic and other unknown reasons, the body attacks self-cells... Rheumatoid arthritis which affects many people in our society is an auto-immune disease.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following are primary lymphoid organs?
questions.explanation: Primary lymphoid organs are bone marrow and thymus where immature lymphocytes differentiate into antigen-sensitive lymphocytes.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the full form of MALT?
questions.explanation: There is lymphoid tissue also located within the lining of the major tracts (respiratory, digestive and urogenital tracts) called mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: AIDS is caused by HIV, which belongs to a group of viruses called:
questions.explanation: AIDS is caused by the Human Immuno deficiency Virus (HIV), a member of a group of viruses called retrovirus.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: HIV enters which cells of the human body and replicates within them?
questions.explanation: Simultaneously, HIV enters into helper T-lymphocytes (TH), replicates and produces progeny viruses.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The most widely used diagnostic test for AIDS is:
questions.explanation: A widely used diagnostic test for AIDS is enzyme linked immuno-sorbent assay (ELISA).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Normal cells show a property by which they inhibit uncontrolled growth through contact with other cells. This property is:
questions.explanation: Normal cells show a property called contact inhibition by virtue of which contact with other cells inhibits their uncontrolled growth.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The property by which cancerous cells slough off from tumors and reach distant sites to start new tumors is called:
questions.explanation: Cells sloughed from such tumors reach distant sites through blood, and where ever they get lodged in the body, they start a new tumor there. This property called metastasis is the most feared property of malignant tumors.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Opioids are drugs that bind to specific opioid receptors present in:
options: Central nervous system and gastrointestinal tract
questions.explanation: Opioids are the drugs, which bind to specific opioid receptors present in our central nervous system and gastrointestinal tract.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Heroin, commonly called smack, is chemically:
questions.explanation: Heroin, commonly called smack is chemically diacetylmorphine which is a white, odourless, bitter crystalline compound.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Cannabinoids are obtained from the inflorescences of the plant:
questions.explanation: Natural cannabinoids are obtained from the inflorescences of the plant Cannabis sativa.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Cocaine, also known as coke or crack, is derived from:
questions.explanation: Coca alkaloid or cocaine is obtained from coca plant Erythroxylum coca.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Tobacco contains an alkaloid that stimulates the adrenal gland to release adrenaline. This alkaloid is:
questions.explanation: Tobacco contains a large number of chemical substances including nicotine, an alkaloid. Nicotine stimulates adrenal gland to release adrenaline and nor-adrenaline.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Chronic use of alcohol can cause severe damage to the liver, a condition known as:
questions.explanation: Chronic use of drugs and alcohol damages nervous system and liver (cirrhosis).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In the life cycle of Plasmodium, sexual stages (gametocytes) develop in:
questions.explanation: Sexual stages (gametocytes) develop in red blood cells (in human).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical barrier in innate immunity?
questions.explanation: Skin on our body is the main barrier which prevents entry of the micro-organisms.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: IgM, IgA, IgD, IgE, and IgG are different types of:
questions.explanation: Different types of antibodies are produced in our body. IgA, IgM, IgE, IgG are some of them.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Vaccination is based on which property of the immune system?
questions.explanation: The principle of immunisation or vaccination is based on the property of ‘memory’ of the immune system.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The chemical substances produced by mast cells during an allergic reaction are:
questions.explanation: Allergy is due to the release of chemicals like histamine and serotonin from the mast cells.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The yellowish fluid colostrum is rich in which antibody?
questions.explanation: The yellowish fluid colostrum... has abundant antibodies (IgA) to protect the infant.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which organ acts as a filter of the blood by trapping blood-borne micro-organisms?
questions.explanation: The spleen is a large bean-shaped organ... It acts as a filter of the blood by trapping blood-borne micro-organisms.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In HIV-infected individuals, the progressive decrease in the number of which cells leads to immune deficiency?
questions.explanation: Due to progressive decrease in the number of helper T lymphocytes, the person starts suffering from infections.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Tobacco smoke contains which gas that reduces the concentration of haembound oxygen in the blood?
questions.explanation: Smoking increases carbon monoxide (CO) content in blood and reduces the concentration of haembound oxygen.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which drug is used for patients who have undergone surgery to relieve pain?
questions.explanation: Morphine is a very effective sedative and painkiller, and is very useful in patients who have undergone surgery.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What happens to the number of RBCs in the blood when the Plasmodium parasites release haemozoin?
questions.explanation: The parasites... attack the red blood cells resulting in their rupture.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Interferons are used in the treatment of:
questions.explanation: ...biological response modifiers such as α-interferon are given which activate their immune system and help in destroying the tumor.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which mosquito species serves as the vector for filariasis?
questions.explanation: The pathogens are transmitted to a healthy person through the bite by the female mosquito vectors (Culex in context of filariasis, though the text simply says female mosquito vectors for the disease).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the common method of transmission for Ascaris?
questions.explanation: Infection takes place through contaminated water, vegetables, fruits, etc.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: During an inflammatory response, which cells act as a cellular barrier in innate immunity?
questions.explanation: Certain types of leukocytes... like polymorpho-nuclear leukocytes (PMNL-neutrophils) and monocytes and natural killer (type of lymphocytes) in the blood as well as macrophages in tissues can phagocytose.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The site where B-lymphocytes and T-lymphocytes interact with antigens and proliferate into effector cells is:
questions.explanation: After maturation the lymphocytes migrate to secondary lymphoid organs... The secondary lymphoid organs provide the sites for interaction of lymphocytes with antigen.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The use of anti-histamine, adrenaline, and steroids quickly reduces the symptoms of:
questions.explanation: The use of drugs like anti-histamine, adrenalin and steroids quickly reduce the symptoms of allergy.
questions.subject: Biology
